title: Why we kill marketing strategy docs after week two
description: The first 60 days decide the year. Here’s the cadence we use to ship them.
content:
Most strategy documents die in a shared Google Drive folder, never opened after week two. We stopped writing them that way.

What we do instead

We ship a one-page operating doc on day one, then update it weekly based on what the data is telling us. No more 40-slide decks that are out of date before the ink is dry.

The first 60 days are about learning, not executing a plan you wrote before you knew anything. Quick wins first — they fund the trust you need for the bigger moves.

title: Demos are a content channel — start treating them that way
description: The unsung pipeline lever for B2B SaaS in 2026.
content:
Every demo your team gives is a compressed, high-intent version of your best content. Most companies let it disappear into a Zoom recording that nobody watches.

Record, clip, distribute

We’ve seen clients 3× their demo-sourced pipeline by simply clipping the best 90 seconds of each call and posting it. The objection-handling sequence is content. The product walkthrough is content. The customer question is content.
